title: Office Corner Desktop
summary: A single-piece corner desktop with one mitered corner cut and a hidden trim grid underneath, designed to fit over two existing storage bases rather than build a whole desk from scratch.
description:
This desktop is designed to drop onto existing furniture — two cabinets, filing units, or bases you already have — rather than being paired with a matching pedestal base like most desks in this catalog. It's really one large square panel with a single corner mitered off at 45°, so the first real task is measuring your own bases and confirming they'll fit the 15-1/2" x 21" opening the plan assumes, adjusting later trim lengths if they don't.

**A trim grid gets built entirely on the underside** of the desktop, invisible once the top is in use — this grid is what actually spans the two bases and gives the corner cut its structural strength, since the panel alone would flex unsupported over that span. The trim pieces attach in a specific order (back edge first, then the pieces flanking the gap where a base sits, then the remaining boards), and the two boards that cross the tabletop's own seam get fastened into both halves for the connection to hold.

A single leg fills the open back corner where no base sits underneath — built from three 1x3 boards glued and clamped into an L or T shape, then screwed up through the tabletop from below. This is the only genuinely load-bearing single point in the whole desk, so let the glue cure fully before loading the corner.

materials.name: 3/4" MDF or hardwood plywood, full sheet
Edge banding, if using hardwood plywood
Wood screws, 3", or corner brackets and screws for attaching the leg
Primer and paint, or stain and poly

cutList.material: 3/4" MDF or hardwood plywood, full sheet
cutList.note: Square panel; one corner cut off in step 1 to form the desktop shape.

cutList.material: 3/4" MDF or hardwood plywood, full sheet
cutList.note: Cut from the corner offcut in step 1.
Cut to measurement, both ends mitered 45°.

steps.title: Measure your bases
steps.body: Measure the tops of the storage bases you plan to use and confirm they fit within a 15-1/2" x 21" opening. If they don't, adjust the trim lengths in the next steps to fit.

steps.title: Cut the tabletop panel
steps.body: Cut the tabletop panel to a 38" x 38" square. Mark 23-1/2" from the back on two adjacent sides, draw a 45° line between the two points, and cut off the corner to form the desktop shape.
materials: 3/4" MDF or hardwood plywood, full sheet

steps.title: Attach the underside trim grid
steps.body: On the underside of the tabletop, fasten the red trim board 3" from the back edge and 1" from the front side edge with 1-1/4" screws and glue. Fasten the green boards next, leaving a 16-1/2" gap between them. Then fasten the blue boards, making sure they span and fasten to both tabletop halves where they cross the seam. Fasten the yellow trim piece, then measure, cut, and glue the pink mitered piece flat while it dries.

steps.title: Build and attach the leg
steps.body: Glue and clamp two of the 1x3 leg pieces together, let dry, then glue and clamp the third piece on. Attach the assembled leg to the tabletop underside with screws or corner brackets through the top.
materials: Wood screws, 3", or corner brackets and screws for attaching the leg

steps.title: Finish
steps.body: Sand all surfaces and edge band any exposed plywood edges if used. Apply primer and paint, or stain and poly.
materials: Edge banding, if using hardwood plywood
Primer and paint, or stain and poly

unresolvedImages.alt: Corner desktop panel with a mitered corner over two storage bases
